findings.evidence: `CircleFundamentalGroup.lean:67-73` proves the general `Multiplicative (zmultiples p)` equivalence; `UniversalCover/AddCircle.lean:139-141` already exposes `Deck.addCircleMulEquiv : Multiplicative (zmultiples p) ≃* Deck ...`; Mathlib’s `AddCircle.isCoveringMap_coe` is also generic over `𝕜` at `Mathlib/Topology/Covering/AddCircle.lean:25-33`.
findings.fix: Make the `zmultiples p` equivalence and its characterization lemmas public, then derive the real nonzero and `UnitAddCircle` ℤ statements as special cases.
findings.issue: `fundamentalGroupMulEquivZMultiples` proves the natural general statement `FundamentalGroup (AddCircle p) x ≃* Multiplicative (zmultiples p)` but is private; the public API starts at line 159 with `p : ℝ`, `hp : p ≠ 0`, and target `Multiplicative ℤ`.

summary: The circle specialization is useful, but the PR hides the natural period-subgroup theorem that the surrounding deck API already supports and then exposes only the nonzero-real/ℤ special case.
